trans-unit.source: Diocese of Madhya Kerala | CMS Press Compound, Kottayam-1

With the blessing of Bishop Rt. Rev. Dr. Malayil Sabu Koshy Cherian, the CSI Counselling Centre offers holistic healing through compassionate, confidential, and evidence-based counselling. Our services are guided by a commitment to mental, emotional, and spiritual well-being.
Personal & online sessions for stress, anxiety, grief, and related challenges.
Resolve conflicts and strengthen family relationships with professional guidance.
Support for behavioral, academic, emotional development, and career guidance.
Assessments for learning difficulties, psychological testing, and career planning.
Three-day residential program to prepare couples for a strong and faithful marriage.
A certified half/one day programme to guide and educate youth preparing for confirmation.
S.H.A.P.E. - Student's Holistic and Pragmatic Empowerment
Empowering individuals to ensure holistic wellbeing through counselling and other initiatives.
Confidential counselling with sensitivity to Christian values.
Panel of certified psychologists, pastoral counsellors, and clergy.
Integration of professional therapy with spiritual care and support.
Both in-person and online appointments at affordable rates.
SHAPE programs, leadership workshops, and mental health campaigns.
A non-judgmental and peaceful setting for personal growth and healing.

To have a pre-marital counselling centre for the CSI Diocese of Madhya Kerala was a felt need addressed at the Diocesan Council meeting of 2006, which was reinforced in the 2007 session. Concrete steps towards this came up with the Bishop Rt. Rev. Thomas Samuel calling up a meeting of counsellors in December 2007. The first major decision was to organise one-day, pre-marital counselling classes in all the twelve clergy districts. The first programme was conducted at the Kottayam clergy district, which was inaugurated by the Diocesan Bishop at Manganam St. John’s CSI Church on 19 January 2008.

In the premarital counselling conducted at Kottayam 125 people attended, while the momentum at the Ettumanoor Clergy District premarital programme was a milestone which saw more than 300 young men and women in attendance. This was then soon spread to other clergy districts too.

In the initial years, the counselling sessions were organised under the auspices of the Bishop Mani Theological Institute, with the participants given BMTI certification. Introduction of two-day residential Pre-Marital camp was a meaningful progress, with three such programmes organised in 2008. From January 2009 onwards, camps organised by BMTI were held every month at the Kottayam CSI Retreat Centre. And the numbers attending these camps came up to even 100.

The inauguration of the CSI Counselling Centre was conducted by the Diocesan Bishop Rt. Rev. Thomas K Oommen on the Diocesan Day 25 July 2011. The Centre was housed in the CMS Press compound. Three-day camps commenced in 2011; the first newsletter “Insight” came out in 2012. The same year saw the publication of the book “Vadhoovaranmaarude Gurukulam” (Couples’ Academic Sanctuary).

Two pre-marital counselling courses were conducted every month from 2014 onwards. The second segment of the three-day camp commenced in January 2014 at Bethel Ashramam, Varikkad (Thiruvalla), which was to happen from the third Thursday to Saturday of each month. From 2017, this was relocated to the Eco-spirituality Centre, Othera. In 2024 the three-day premarital camp was fixed to conducted only in the first Thursday to Saturday every month at CSI retreat centre Kottayam.

The centre started Counselling with focus on individuals, schools and communities with classes in ten selected schools. Regular programmes were organised at the Kottayam CNI TTI and Baker Memorial Girls Higher Secondary School. In 2019-21 introduced The SHAPE (Students’ Holistic and Pragmatic Empowerment) programme as the students’ empowerment programme.

From 11th August 2025 onwards, a joint venture between the CSI Counseling Centre, Kottayam, under the SHAPE (Students Holistic and Pragmatic Empowerment) Project, and the Department of Psychology, Baker College for Women, Kottayam inaugurated an Add-On Programme in Counselling Psychology.

On 6 September 2025, inauguration of our Counselling Centre web app by the esteemed Bishop, the Rt. Revd. Dr. Malayil Sabu Koshy Cherian. Where the new digital platform greatly enhance ability to provide ease to registration and accessible support.

The inaugural Pre-confirmational Counselling session was successfully held at St. Thomas CSI Church, Kollam, on October 18, 2025. A total of 14 candidates participated and greatly benefitted from the guidance. Which followed by other churches of our diocese. The Pre-confirmational Counselling focuses on equipping our teenagers who are preparing for confirmation with essential life skills. The unique counselling program goes beyond the doctrines to focus on Self-Awareness, Healthy Friendships, Healthy relationships and Future Orientation. It gives our teen the tools they need to live their faith powerfully in a changing world.

The Diocesan Counselling Centre continues its ministry as a guide and support to many, acting as a mentoring space for couples and individuals, thus attempting to fulfil its call to counsel to the mind and care for the soul.

Enter your registered email to access Feedback and Questionnaire Forms
You must register to access these forms.
An error occurred while verifying your email. Please try again later.
The feedback and questionnaire forms are only accessible during your session period.
Please select a form to proceed.
Share your thoughts and opinions to help us understand participants better.
What did you find most valuable about this session?
What is your definition of marriage?
What wishes and concerns do you have about marriage?
How did your parent’s physical and mental health relate to you in growing up?
Suppose the woman were the eldest of sisters/brothers and the man was the youngest child in his family and had elder sisters. How will that affect the marriage?
What are you expecting from your would-be partner?
Your understanding about sex? Who talked with you?
What fears/anxieties do you have about marriage?
How much time can you spend with your life partner after the marriage?
Advantages and disadvantages of the age differences?
How would your relationship with your parents and in-laws be during the early years of your marriage?
What will be the greatest adjustment when you are in a new home? What is your approach about family prayer and Sunday church service after your marriage?
Your responses have been submitted successfully!
“Come to me, all who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.”

A 3-day program to prepare couples for a committed and faithful marriage.
Offline Premarital Course registration specifically for candidates residing outside Kerala and abroad. The session is hosted by the parish on behalf of the Counselling Centre, Kottayam.
Counselling support for individuals, families, and students.
One-day counselling program for youth preparing for confirmation.

I hereby make my full consent that I have read and fully understand the information provided in this consent form. I voluntarily agree to participate in the counseling sessions and commit to the policies and responsibilities outlined above.
I have read and agree to the Informed Consent and Counseling Agreement
Read and Accept Informed Consent and Counselling Agreement
Informed Consent for Premarital Counseling Camp
1. Nature and Goals of Premarital Counseling
This premarital counseling camp is a structured program by CSI MADHYA KERALA DIOCESE designed to help couples build a strong, healthy foundation for their marriage. Our goals include:
Exploring expectations about marriage, finances, roles, and family.
Identifying and discussing core values and beliefs.
Providing tools and strategies for a lifelong partnership.

The camp involves group sessions, couple activities, and educational workshops led by qualified counselors. It is not intended to be a substitute for individual or intensive couples therapy for severe issues.

Confidentiality is a cornerstone of our work. Everything you share in your private sessions with a counselor will remain confidential. However, there are legal and ethical limits to this confidentiality:
Threat of Harm: If a counselor believes a participant is at risk of harming themselves or another person, they are legally and ethically obligated to intervene to ensure safety.
Abuse or Neglect: Suspicions of child, elder, or dependent adult abuse or neglect must be reported to the appropriate authorities.
Court Order: If a court orders the release of records, the counselor must comply.
In group settings, we ask all participants to respect the privacy of others and maintain confidentiality by not sharing what is discussed outside of the group.
Please arrive at the camp location by 9:15 AM on Thursday, as registration begins at that time.
All sessions/classes will begin at 10:00 AM.
Bring all necessary personal items for your stay from Thursday (9:30 AM) to Saturday (10:30 AM), such as a blanket, toothbrush, toothpaste, soap, towels, Bible, etc.
Participants are not permitted to leave the camp premises during the program.
Parents are required to attend the final session on Saturday from 9:00 AM to 10:30 AM.
After registration closes on the Monday prior to the camp, a WhatsApp group will be created for further communication.

Attendance: Full participation in all scheduled sessions and activities is expected to get the most out of the experience. Be actively involved—attend, interact, participate, and engage during the camp.

Fees: The cost for the premarital counseling camp is fixed as per the decision of core committee of the CSI COUNSELLING CENTER KOTTAYAM. This fee covers all workshops, materials, food, stay and if any private session with a counselor. The camp fee must be paid in cash at the registration office upon arrival.
Cancellation Policy: Cancellations made at least 1 week before the start of the camp. No refunds will be issued for cancellations made after this date.
Your Rights: You have the right to ask questions about the counseling process, to decide what you wish to share in the camp at any time.

Your Responsibilities: To ensure a successful experience, we ask that you commit to active and open participation, treating all counselors and fellow participants with respect, and being willing to explore difficult topics honestly with your partner.

I hereby make my full consent that I have read and fully understand the information provided in this consent form. I voluntarily agree to participate in the premarital counseling camp and commit to the policies and responsibilities outlined above.
With a panel of certified counsellors, psychologists, and clergy dedicated to your holistic healing.
